claim_status: No-claim platform-source manifest only; platform documentation is not Turing sandbox evidence, effective-policy capture, containment proof, security-gate approval, production-safety evidence, or implementation evidence.

sources.title: AppContainer isolation
sources.scope: Process, file, registry, network, device, credential, and brokered-access isolation model.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture effective token, package and capability identity, integrity level, ACLs, inherited handles, broker path, and granted resource set for the exact packaged role.

sources.title: Create Process In Sandbox APIs
sources.scope: Parameterized sandbox-launch fields for AppContainer, integrity, capabilities, filesystem, network, UI restrictions, and process mitigations.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture effective AppContainer mode and all applied launch restrictions; requested fields must not be treated as effective when the underlying isolation mode is disabled, and the API documentation is not a Turing containment result.

sources.title: Process mitigation policy
sources.scope: Effective process mitigation policy, including dynamic-code, image-load, child-process, and control-flow restrictions.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture queried effective mitigations and distinguish an expected denial from a role that fails to launch or silently falls back.

sources.title: Seccomp BPF
sources.scope: System-call filtering as one restriction layer, not a complete sandbox or information-flow policy.
sources.probe_consequence: Record filter hash, architecture checks, no-new-privileges state, namespaces, filesystem policy, broker rules, and residual surfaces; a syscall denial alone cannot pass PB-012.

sources.title: Landlock
sources.scope: Stackable unprivileged filesystem and, depending on kernel ABI, network restrictions.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture kernel identity, detected ABI, handled rights, unsupported rights, ruleset digest, and degraded behavior rather than assuming a fixed policy.

sources.title: User namespaces and resource control
sources.scope: Resource-misuse implications of user namespaces and the recommendation to use memory control groups when they are enabled.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture namespace limits and cgroup/resource-control state with the user namespace; namespace creation alone is not bounded-resource or containment evidence.

sources.title: App Sandbox
sources.scope: Entitlement-based restriction of system resources and user data.
sources.probe_consequence: Bind signed executable identity, entitlement set, container state, helper relationship, inherited descriptors, broker path, and unavailable-entitlement behavior to the tested role.

sources.title: Hardened Runtime
sources.scope: Code-signing and runtime restrictions relevant to library validation, executable memory, debugging, and JIT policy.
sources.probe_consequence: Capture signature and hardened-runtime state, library-validation and JIT exceptions, loaded-library policy, and negative debug or code-loading results.

unsupported: No effective platform policy has been captured.
No packaged expected-deny probe has been executed.
No sandbox, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing, security-gate, production-safety, or implementation claim exists.
